rationale.https://docs.python.org/3/library/asynci: Official Python asyncio documentation (top-level, v3.14) — THE authoritative reference. Primary source for all asyncio questions.
Official Python event loop documentation — directly on-topic for 'event loop' component. Covers policy system, running loops, scheduling callbacks.
Official asyncio development guide — covers debugging, subprocess, concurrency model best practices. Practical companion to the API docs.
rationale.https://realpython.com/async-io-python/: Real Python asyncio walkthrough — one of the most-cited practical asyncio tutorials. Covers coroutines, event loops, tasks, I/O-bound concurrency. Well-maintained.
rationale.https://pymotw.com/3/asyncio/index.html: PyMOTW (Python Module of the Week) asyncio — comprehensive module walkthrough by Doug Hellmann. Covers event loop, coroutines, tasks, transport, protocol. Authoritative.
rationale.https://lucumr.pocoo.org/2016/10/30/i-do: Armin Ronacher (Flask creator) on asyncio internals — famous deep-dive post explaining the event loop architecture. Addresses 'what is actually happening' level of understanding.
rationale.https://async-concurrency.com/asyncio-fu: Production asyncio event loop configuration guide — specifically addresses high-concurrency workloads, thread starvation, deterministic shutdown. Highly practical for 'concurrency' dimension.
rationale.https://asyncio-notes.readthedocs.io/en/: asyncio developer notes event loops documentation — focuses on the policy pattern, event loop management model. Directly on-topic for event loop internals.
rationale.https://tutorialedge.net/python/concurre: asyncio event loops tutorial — tutorial specifically about the event loop component, covering asyncio.get_event_loop() and the event loop lifecycle.
rationale.https://doi.org/10.1007/978-1-4842-4401-: 'Working with Event Loops' — Apress book chapter specifically about asyncio event loops and event loop policies. Academic/textbook treatment of event loops.

notes: Pool of 40 has strong on-topic signal at the top (official Python docs, canonical tutorials) but significant noise: Julia for HEP (completely off-topic), Xilinx RFSoC/SDR paper, automated driving digital twins, Power Hardware-in-the-Loop, Smart City IoT paper, Nightcore serverless microservices. Also 6 Springer chapters from asyncio books (de Groot 2022, Hunt 2019/2023, Hetland 2024, Divakaran 2025) — relevant but less accessible than tutorial/official sources. The aosabook.org crawler chapter (AOSA) is a strong alternative #11, excluded only by top-10 constraint.
